title: Monetary Policy Implementation

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is an educational page from the New York Fed describing the general framework for implementing monetary policy, including the mechanisms for controlling the federal funds rate (IORB, ON RRP, SRP), open market operations, and additional liquidity tools. It is not a report of a specific monetary policy decision, data release, or market event.
key_points: The Federal Reserve controls the federal funds rate primarily through the interest rate on reserve balances (IORB), which sets a floor for bank lending rates

Overnight Reverse Repo (ON RRP) operations provide a floor under overnight money market rates for eligible counterparties including money market funds, GSEs, primary dealers, and depository institutions
Standing Repo (SRP) operations provide a ceiling on overnight money market rates and are expected to be actively used when economically sensible
The discount window provides liquidity to banks through Primary Credit, available to institutions in generally sound financial condition with eligible collateral
The FIMA Repo Facility allows approved foreign central banks to enter overnight repo transactions against Treasury securities held at the New York Fed
The federal funds rate is the interest rate on overnight unsecured transactions between banks for reserve balances held at the Federal Reserve
why_it_matters: This page describes the operational infrastructure that underpins federal funds rate control and market plumbing. Understanding IORB, ON RRP, and SRP mechanics is essential for comprehending how Fed policy decisions transmit to money markets. However, this is educational background material rather than a record of a specific policy event or market development. The source lacks a publication date and URL, limiting its utility as a dated archival record.
market_structure_context: This page directly describes market structure elements including: the Fed's administered rates framework (IORB), standing facilities (ON RRP, SRP), the discount window, and FIMA repo facility. It explains how the Desk executes open market operations and how these tools maintain the federal funds rate within the target range.
